title: Chewing Gum on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within 24 hours; freeze + lift only at home
stainChemistry: Chewing gum is hydrophobic polymer + sweeteners + softeners. Mechanical adhesion to fiber, not chemical bond. On wool, gum sticks AND wool is the fiber that worst tolerates the chemistry that lifts gum: NO citrus solvent floods (Goo Gone needs water rinse, water + wool = felt), NO oily DIY tricks (peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo leave grease halo on wool that's harder than the gum), NO hot water (felts wool), NO scrubbing (felts wool on contact). The freeze-and-lift approach IS safe on wool — cold + mechanical removal without chemistry. Pro uses controlled citrus solvent + cold blot for residue.
whyThisWorks: Cold makes gum brittle, lifts off mechanically without water or solvent flood. Wool tolerates cold + mechanical lift. Pro applies controlled citrus solvent + cold blot for residual film — chemistry that's wool-tuned. Home cannot match pro solvent application without flood = felt risk. Freeze-and-lift + pro escalation is the wool-safe path.
defaultAssumption: Wool + chewing gum = freeze-and-lift at home, pro within 24 hours for residual. Fresh gum is easier than dried.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Place ice in sealed bag on the gum. 10–15 min until brittle. Never direct ice on wool — water damage AND felt risk.
Crack the frozen gum with the FLAT edge of a plastic card. Lift pieces off. Don't scrape wool — flat-edge lift only. Refreeze if it softens. Most bulk comes off.

spottingProtocol.technique: Citrus solvent applied to cloth, no flood
spottingProtocol.instruction: Pro applies Goo Gone to a white cloth (NEVER pour on wool). Tight blotting on residual gum film. Flip cloth as it picks up. Ventilate. No flooding wool.
Cold NSD sponge press for solvent residue. Press only, no agitation.
Press between dry towels. Lay flat to air-dry, reshape damp.

steps: 2. Crack and lift bulk with flat-edge plastic card.
3. Goo Gone on cloth, tight blotting, no flood.
4. Cold wool-safe NSD sponge press for solvent residue.
products: Goo Gone (citrus solvent, pro-applied to cloth)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold (freeze) + ambient solvent + cold rinse — no heat
warnings: NEVER pour solvent on wool — flood + agitation = felt
NEVER agitation / scrubbing — felts wool on contact
NEVER peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo / oil tricks — leave grease halo on wool worse than gum

homeSolutions.agent: Freeze and Lift Is Safe; Oily DIY Tricks Damage Wool
homeSolutions.instruction: Wool + chewing gum is one of the simpler home cases — IF you do nothing but freeze and lift. The internet's worst advice for gum: peanut butter, WD-40, mayo, hair conditioner. Those soften gum, but on wool they leave a GREASE HALO that's harder to remove than the gum, AND the rubbing-it-in step felts wool. Stick to freeze + flat-edge lift. Pro handles residue.

homeSolutions.agent: Freeze the Gum Brittle — 10 to 15 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Put ice in a zip-lock bag and press it directly on the gum for 10–15 minutes. NEVER put ice directly on the wool — water damage AND wet wool + agitation = felt. Wool is forgiving of pressure-on-bag-on-wool, but not of moisture + friction. The gum will get hard and brittle.

homeSolutions.agent: Crack and Lift — Flat Edge of Plastic Card
homeSolutions.instruction: Once the gum is brittle, use the FLAT edge of a plastic card (gift card, credit card) or a dull butter knife to crack and lift the gum in pieces. Don't scrape — wool fibers shred under any sharp edge. Flat-edge lift only. If the gum softens before you finish, refreeze.

homeSolutions.agent: Press Dry — DO NOT Apply Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: After lifting bulk, press the area with a dry white towel. No water, no solvent, no soap at home — those add felt risk. If a thin film of gum residue remains, that's pro territory.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Wool Specialist for Residual — What to Tell Them
homeSolutions.instruction: Take to a wool specialist (call ahead). Tell them: "Chewing gum on wool, [fresh / hardened], freeze-and-lift at home. Bulk lifted with plastic card flat edge. NOTHING applied — no Goo Gone, no peanut butter, no WD-40, no water, no soap." Pros use controlled Goo Gone or POG applied to cloth (NOT poured on wool) + cold NSD sponge press for residual film.

steps: 1. Wool + chewing gum = freeze + lift at home, pro for residue.
2. NO peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo / conditioner / oil tricks.
3. Freeze with ice-in-bag 10–15 min. Never direct ice on wool.
4. Crack and lift with flat-edge plastic card. No scraping.
5. Press dry with white towel. No solvent or soap at home.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after lifting bulk. Home solvents on wool flood and felt the fiber.
warnings: NEVER peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo / hair conditioner on wool — grease halo
NEVER pour Goo Gone on wool — flood + felt risk
NEVER direct ice on wool — water damage
NEVER scrub or rub wet wool
Pro handles residual film, not home

neverDo: Never oily DIY tricks (PB / WD-40 / mayo / conditioner) on wool
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
Wool tolerates dry mechanical lift (cold + flat edge) safely
Solvent flood on wool = felt risk
Oily home tricks leave grease halo harder than the gum

materialWarnings: Freeze + lift = the safe home path
Oily DIY tricks DESTROY wool worse than gum
Solvent flood on wool = felt risk

escalation.when: Residual film after freeze + lift, or anyone has applied oil-based DIY (peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo / conditioner).
escalation.whatToTell: "Chewing gum on wool, [fresh / hardened], age [hours / days], freeze-and-lift at home with plastic card flat edge. NOTHING applied — no Goo Gone, no peanut butter, no oil, no water, no soap [or: someone applied X before I got here]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead — confirm controlled Goo Gone / POG on cloth + wool-safe protocol)

contextQuestions: How long ago — minutes, hours, or days?
Has anyone tried peanut butter / WD-40 / mayo / Goo Gone?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Hardened gum lifts cleaner than fresh soft gum. Pre-applied oily DIY = grease halo present, pro will see it; honest disclosure helps. Pre-applied Goo Gone = wool likely partially flooded — possible felt risk.
